title: stenochlaena pittieri
intro: Stenochlaena pittieri, also known as Pittier's maidenhair or Pittier's fern, is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. It is native to New Caledonia and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Stenochlaena pittieri include reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and relieving congestion, making it a popular herbal remedy. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues.
